html:
[1]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 《独异志》者，记世事之独异也 - A formal introduction explaining that *The Book of Unique Events* records extraordinary events in the world.
  • 自开辟以来迄于今世之经籍 - A phrase indicating that the book covers events from the beginning of time to the present day.
  • 耳目可见闻 - Means 'what can be seen and heard,' referring to observable and audible phenomena.
  • 神仙鬼怪 - Refers to immortals, ghosts, and monsters, common subjects in Chinese folklore.
  • 纪载所繁者 - Means 'those that are extensively recorded,' referring to well-documented events.
  • 构成三卷 - Means 'composed into three volumes,' indicating the structure of the book.
  • 愿传博达，所贵解颜耳 - Means 'I hope it will be passed on to the learned, for the sake of bringing smiles,' expressing the author's wish for the book to be appreciated.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 伊尹无父，生于空桑中 - Refers to Yi Yin, a famous minister of the Shang dynasty, who was said to have been born without a father and from a mulberry tree.
  • 禹妻化为石，后剖腹而生启 - Refers to the myth of Yu the Great's wife transforming into a stone and giving birth to Qi (启) by cutting open her belly.
  • 老君耳长七尺 - Refers to Laozi (老子), the founder of Daoism, who is said to have had ears seven feet long, a symbol of wisdom.
  • 徐偃王无骨而有圣德 - Refers to Xu Yanzhong (徐偃王), a legendary king known for his virtue despite being boneless.
  • 刘邕好食人疮痂 - Refers to Liu Yong (刘邕), a person who had a peculiar habit of eating scabs, which is considered unusual and unhygienic.
  • 文王四乳 - Refers to Wen Wang (文王), a virtuous ruler of the Zhou dynasty, who is said to have had four breasts, a sign of his exceptional nature.
  • 皋陶鸟喙 - Refers to Gao Yao (皋陶), a legendary judge of the Xia dynasty, who had a beak-like mouth, a physical anomaly.
  • 尧眉八彩 - Refers to Yao (尧), a legendary ruler, whose eyebrows were said to have eight colors, symbolizing his divine qualities.
  • 汤臂四肘 - Refers to Tang (汤), a famous ruler of the Shang dynasty, who is said to have had four elbows on each arm, a physical anomaly.
  • 禹耳三漏 - Refers to Yu the Great, who had three holes in his ears, a physical anomaly.
  • 离娄察见秋毫于十里之外 - Refers to Li Lou (离娄), a legendary figure with extremely sharp eyesight, able to see fine details from a great distance.
  • 卫臣弘演开已腹纳懿公之肝 - Refers to the story of a minister of the State of Wei who cut open his own belly to hide the liver of Duke Yi Gong (懿公), a ruler who was overthrown.
  • 周穆贵为天子，车辙马迹遍于天下 - Refers to King Mu of Zhou (周穆王), a legendary ruler whose travels were so extensive that his chariot tracks and horse prints were said to cover the entire world.
  • 海若居海岛，每至八月即有流槎过 - Refers to the myth of Hai Ruo (海若), a sea deity, and the annual appearance of a log raft in the sea during the eighth month of the lunar calendar.
  • 其人赍粮乘槎而往 - Means 'the person carried provisions and went aboard the raft,' describing the journey.
  • 饮牛于河 - Means 'drinking water from the river,' a simple act that provides context for the setting.
  • 织女 - Refers to the Weaving Maid, a celestial figure in Chinese mythology.
  • 可归问蜀严君平，当知之 - Means 'you can return and ask Shu's Yan Junping, he will know,' suggesting a way to find out more information.
  • 某年月日，有客星犯斗牛 - Refers to the appearance of a guest star (a comet or nova) in the constellations of Dou (斗) and Niu (牛), a significant astronomical event.

Proper Nouns

  • 《独异志》 - The title of the book, *The Book of Unique Events*.
  • 李亢 - The author of the book, Li Kang.
  • 伊尹 - Yi Yin, a famous minister of the Shang dynasty.
  • 禹 - Yu the Great, a legendary ruler known for his flood control efforts.
  • 老君 - Laozi, the founder of Daoism.
  • 徐偃王 - Xu Yanzhong, a legendary king.
  • 刘邕 - Liu Yong, a person with a peculiar habit.
  • 文王 - Wen Wang, a virtuous ruler of the Zhou dynasty.
  • 皋陶 - Gao Yao, a legendary judge of the Xia dynasty.
  • 尧 - Yao, a legendary ruler.
  • 汤 - Tang, a famous ruler of the Shang dynasty.
  • 离娄 - Li Lou, a legendary figure with sharp eyesight.
  • 卫臣弘演 - A minister of the State of Wei.
  • 懿公 - Duke Yi Gong, a ruler of the State of Wei.
  • 海若 - Hai Ruo, a sea deity.
  • 严君平 - Yan Junping, a scholar from Shu.
  • 织女 - The Weaving Maid, a celestial figure.

[2]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 娥皇、女英从舜巡狩 - Refers to the legendary consorts of Emperor Shun, who accompanied him on his travels.
  • 行及湘川 - Means 'when they reached the Xiang River.'
  • 闻舜崩于苍梧 - Means 'they heard that Shun had died in Cangwu (a place in present-day Guangxi).'
  • 泣下，泪洒湘川之竹，皆成斑文 - Means 'they wept, and their tears fell on the bamboo of the Xiang River, which turned into spotted patterns.' This is a metaphor for the weeping of the bamboo.
  • 公孙吕面长三尺，阔三寸 - Describes the unusual appearance of Gong Sun Lü, a minister of the State of Wei during the Spring and Autumn period.
  • 为卫国贤臣 - Means 'he was a virtuous minister of the State of Wei.'
  • 东汉赵伯翁尝昼寝 - Means 'during the Eastern Han Dynasty, Zhao Bo翁 once took a nap during the day.'
  • 群孙戏其腹上 - Means 'his grandchildren played on his stomach.'
  • 内七李于脐中 - Means 'they put seven plums into his navel.'
  • 李至烂，流汁出 - Means 'the plums rotted, and the juice flowed out.'
  • 其家谓其将死 - Means 'his family thought he was going to die.'
  • 后李核出而无患 - Means 'later, the plum pits came out, and he had no problem.'
  • 刘曜字永明，须百茎，皆长五尺 - Describes Liu Yao, a Western Jin Dynasty official, whose beard had a hundred strands, each five feet long.
  • 汉中山靖王胜，有男女一百人 - Means 'Emperor Jing of Zhongshan during the Han Dynasty had one hundred sons and daughters.'
  • 其后子孙流衍于今 - Means 'his descendants have spread out until today.'
  • 问之，皆刘裔 - Means 'when asked, they are all descendants of Liu.'
  • 后周独孤信三女为后 - Means 'in the Northern Zhou Dynasty, three daughters of Dugu Xin became empresses.'
  • 各生周、隋、唐一朝天子 - Means 'each gave birth to an emperor of the Zhou, Sui, and Tang Dynasties.'
  • 长生周武帝，次生隋炀帝，次生唐高祖 - Means 'the eldest gave birth to Emperor Wu of Zhou, the second to Emperor Yang of Sui, and the third to Emperor Gaozu of Tang.'
  • 汉昌邑王贺即位二十七日，积恶凡一千四百二十七条 - Means 'Emperor Changyi of Han reigned for twenty-seven days and accumulated a total of one thousand four hundred and twenty-seven evils.'
  • 为霍光所废 - Means 'he was deposed by Huo Guang.'
  • 项羽每叱咤，万人手足皆废 - Means 'every time Xiang Yu shouted, ten thousand people's hands and feet would fall off.' This is a hyperbolic description of his intimidating presence.
  • 晋宣王司马懿，自顾见背 - Means 'King Xuan of Jin, Sima Yi, looked back and saw himself being abandoned.' This is a metaphorical expression indicating his sense of betrayal or loss.
  • 隋有麦铁杖，一夕行一千百里 - Means 'during the Sui Dynasty, Mai Tietang could travel one thousand miles in one night.'
  • 夕发洛阳往宋州为盗，及明却返 - Means 'he left Luoyang at night to go to Song Prefecture to steal, and returned by morning.'
  • 宋人因见其所盗之物者，执麦告之，为吏所劫，乃承愆 - Means 'the people of Song saw the things he stole and arrested Mai, but he was rescued by officials, and he confessed his guilt.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 娥皇、女英 - Legendary consorts of Emperor Shun, known for their loyalty and sorrow after his death.
  • 舜 - A legendary Chinese emperor and the second of the Three Sovereigns and Five Emperors.
  • 苍梧 - A place in present-day Guangxi, where Emperor Shun is said to have died.
  • 公孙吕 - A minister of the State of Wei during the Spring and Autumn period, known for his unusual appearance.
  • 卫国 - One of the major states during the Spring and Autumn period.
  • 赵伯翁 - A figure from the Eastern Han Dynasty, whose story is used to illustrate a miraculous event.
  • 刘曜 - A Western Jin Dynasty official, known for his long beard.
  • 汉中山靖王胜 - Emperor Jing of Zhongshan during the Han Dynasty, known for having many children.
  • 后周 - The Northern Zhou Dynasty, which ruled northern China in the 6th century.
  • 独孤信 - A prominent general and politician during the Northern Zhou Dynasty, whose daughters became empresses.
  • 昌邑王贺 - Emperor Changyi of Han, who was deposed by Huo Guang.
  • 霍光 - A powerful regent during the Western Han Dynasty, who deposed Emperor Changyi.
  • 项羽 - A famous warlord and rival of Liu Bang during the Chu-Han Contention.
  • 晋宣王司马懿 - A key figure in the founding of the Jin Dynasty, known for his political acumen.
  • 麦铁杖 - A legendary figure from the Sui Dynasty, known for his incredible speed and ability to evade capture.
  • 宋州 - A prefecture in present-day Henan, where Mai Tietang is said to have committed his theft.

Proper Nouns

  • 娥皇、女英 - Legendary consorts of Emperor Shun.
  • 舜 - A legendary Chinese emperor.
  • 苍梧 - A place in present-day Guangxi.
  • 公孙吕 - A minister of the State of Wei.
  • 卫国 - One of the major states during the Spring and Autumn period.
  • 赵伯翁 - A figure from the Eastern Han Dynasty.
  • 刘曜 - A Western Jin Dynasty official.
  • 汉中山靖王胜 - Emperor Jing of Zhongshan during the Han Dynasty.
  • 后周 - The Northern Zhou Dynasty.
  • 独孤信 - A prominent general and politician during the Northern Zhou Dynasty.
  • 昌邑王贺 - Emperor Changyi of Han.
  • 霍光 - A powerful regent during the Western Han Dynasty.
  • 项羽 - A famous warlord and rival of Liu Bang.
  • 晋宣王司马懿 - A key figure in the founding of the Jin Dynasty.
  • 麦铁杖 - A legendary figure from the Sui Dynasty.
  • 宋州 - A prefecture in present-day Henan.

[3]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 三年間為水火焚蕩俱盡 - Literally 'over three years, all was burned and destroyed by water and fire,' used to describe total destruction.
  • 連八年孿生十六子 - Literally 'for eight consecutive years, twins were born, totaling sixteen children,' an unusual and exaggerated description of fertility.
  • 三舍身 - A Buddhist practice where one renounces worldly life to become a monk, here referring to Emperor Wu of Liang's act of becoming a monk.
  • 考二十四考 - A term referring to a high-ranking official's long service record, with '考' meaning 'term of office.'
  • 月入俸錢二萬貫 - Refers to a monthly salary of 20,000 strings of coins, a very high amount.
  • 官供二千人 - Means 'officially supported by 2,000 people,' likely referring to a large retinue or household.
  • 熟食、馬料五百石 - Refers to 500 shi of cooked food and horse fodder, a large quantity.
  • 貴極 - Means 'reached the highest rank or status.'
  • 綿聯八朝帝王 - Literally 'connected to eight dynasties of emperors,' referring to Dehuang's influence across multiple dynasties.
  • 拔佩刀刺山石 - Literally 'pulled out his sword and stabbed the mountain stone,' a dramatic action.
  • 泉涌 - Means 'springs forth,' describing the result of the action.
  • 身重千斤 - Literally 'weighs a thousand catties,' an exaggeration used to describe extreme obesity.
  • 陛下秤上秤臣 - Literally 'Your Majesty, weigh me on the scale,' a request for a demonstration.
  • 雖肉重千斤，而智無一兩 - A play on words, meaning 'though my flesh weighs a thousand catties, my wisdom weighs not even an ounce.'
  • 潛推一婢於墓中 - Literally 'secretly pushed a maid into the tomb,' a mysterious and unusual action.
  • 開墓，婢伏於棺上，久而乃生 - Literally 'when the tomb was opened, the maid was found lying on the coffin, and after a long time, she revived.'
  • 染翰欲書 - Literally 'dipped the brush to write,' a poetic description of writing.
  • 終不及牆 - Literally 'never reached the wall,' indicating a long walk without finding a place to write.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 淮南程干 - Refers to Cheng Gan, a wealthy man from Huainan.
  • 梁武帝 - Emperor Wu of Liang, a famous emperor known for his promotion of Buddhism.
  • 唐郭子儀 - Guo Ziyi, a prominent general and statesman of the Tang Dynasty.
  • 中書令 - A high-ranking official position in the Tang Dynasty.
  • 郭太后 - Dehuang, a powerful empress who influenced multiple Tang emperors.
  • 李廣利 - Li Guangli, a general of the Han Dynasty known for his military exploits.
  • 東漢孟業 - Meng Ye, a figure from the Eastern Han Dynasty known for his unusual physical condition.
  • 干寶 - Gan Bao, a historian and writer of the Eastern Jin Dynasty.
  • 維摩詰方丈室 - Refers to the room of Vimalakirti, a famous figure in Buddhist literature.

Proper Nouns

  • 淮南 - A region in present-day Anhui Province.
  • 程干 - Cheng Gan, a wealthy man from Huainan.
  • 梁武帝 - Emperor Wu of Liang.
  • 唐郭子儀 - Guo Ziyi.
  • 中書令 - A high-ranking official position.
  • 郭太后 - Dehuang.
  • 李廣利 - Li Guangli.
  • 東漢孟業 - Meng Ye.
  • 干寶 - Gan Bao.
  • 維摩詰 - Vimalakirti, a famous figure in Buddhist literature.

[4]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 無疾而終 - Literally 'died without illness,' often used to describe a peaceful or natural death.
  • 不離君之身 - A phrase suggesting a close, unbreakable bond, possibly implying a spiritual or metaphysical connection.
  • 更與君為親 - Literally 'will become your kin again,' suggesting a future reunion or transformation.
  • 前生之事 - Refers to events or memories from a previous life, invoking the concept of reincarnation.
  • 異長 - Literally 'abnormal growth,' used to describe a sudden and unusual physical change.
  • 頭須皓白 - Describes a sudden graying or whitening of hair, often used to symbolize aging or shock.
  • 戴竿 - Refers to a performance where a person balances on a pole, often seen as a form of acrobatic or folk art.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 唐柳子升 - A historical figure from the Tang Dynasty, though his exact identity or significance is unclear from the text.
  • 前梁張軌 - Refers to Zhang Gui, a military governor during the Western Jin Dynasty, known for his role in the Xiliang region.
  • 抱罕令 - 'Bao Han' is likely a place name, possibly referring to a region in present-day Gansu Province.
  • 魏建凌云阁 - Refers to the construction of the 'Lingyun Pavilion' during the Wei Dynasty, a historical architectural project.
  • 文帝 - Likely refers to Cao Pi, the first emperor of the Wei Dynasty.
  • 贾弼之 - A historical figure, possibly a scholar or official, though his exact role is unclear.
  • 唐惠卿 - A historical figure, possibly a scholar or official, though his exact role is unclear.
  • 德宗朝 - Refers to the reign of Emperor Dezong of the Tang Dynasty (779–805).
  • 三原妇人王大娘 - A historical figure, likely a performer or entertainer, known for her skill in balancing on a pole.

Proper Nouns

  • 唐柳子升 - A historical figure from the Tang Dynasty.
  • 前梁張軌 - Zhang Gui, a military governor during the Western Jin Dynasty.
  • 抱罕令 - A place name, possibly referring to a region in present-day Gansu Province.
  • 魏建凌云阁 - The 'Lingyun Pavilion' during the Wei Dynasty.
  • 文帝 - Cao Pi, the first emperor of the Wei Dynasty.
  • 贾弼之 - A historical figure, possibly a scholar or official.
  • 唐惠卿 - A historical figure, possibly a scholar or official.
  • 德宗朝 - The reign of Emperor Dezong of the Tang Dynasty.
  • 三原妇人王大娘 - A historical figure, likely a performer or entertainer.

[5]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 韶阳 - A place name, likely referring to a region or city in ancient China.
  • 一旦 - Means 'one day' or 'suddenly.'
  • 舐其臂 - Literally 'licked his arm,' but in context, it implies a supernatural or unusual event.
  • 色皎白 - Means 'color as white as jade,' suggesting a miraculous or divine transformation.
  • 暴卒 - Means 'sudden death.'
  • 恨 - Means 'to hate' or 'to resent,' indicating the family's anger.
  • 召村社同食之 - Means 'called the village community to eat it together,' suggesting a communal or ritualistic act.
  • 凡食者数十人 - Means 'all who ate it were dozens of people,' indicating a widespread effect.
  • 一夕同卒 - Means 'all died on the same night,' emphasizing the sudden and collective nature of the deaths.
  • 羿烧仙药 - Refers to the mythological figure Yi, who burned elixirs of immortality.
  • 药成 - Means 'the medicine was completed.'
  • 窃而食之 - Means 'stole and ate it,' indicating a secretive or forbidden act.
  • 遂奔入月中 - Means 'then fled into the moon,' suggesting a transformation or escape into the celestial realm.
  • 《山海经》有大耳国 - Refers to the *Shanhaijing* (Classic of Mountains and Seas), an ancient text containing mythological and geographical descriptions.
  • 寝 - Means 'to sleep,' but in this context, it refers to a unique sleeping posture.
  • 常以一耳为席，一耳为衾 - Literally 'often used one ear as a pillow and the other as a quilt,' suggesting a bizarre or mythical practice.
  • 贾耽为滑州节度 - Refers to the historical figure Jia Dan, who served as the military governor of Huazhou.
  • 酸枣县有俚妇事姑不敬 - Refers to a woman in Suzao County who was disrespectful to her mother-in-law.
  • 无双目 - Means 'no eyes,' indicating blindness.
  • 旦食 - Means 'morning meal.'
  • 以食裹纳犬粪授姑 - Means 'wrapped dog feces in food and gave it to her mother-in-law,' indicating a cruel act.
  • 觉有异气 - Means 'felt something strange,' suggesting suspicion or discomfort.
  • 仰天大哭 - Means 'cried to the sky,' indicating a dramatic or emotional reaction.
  • 雷电发 - Means 'thunder and lightning struck,' suggesting divine intervention.
  • 若有人截妇首，以犬续之 - Literally 'as if someone cut off the woman's head and replaced it with a dog's,' indicating a grotesque punishment.
  • 牵行于境内 - Means 'dragged her body through the territory,' suggesting a public display or punishment.
  • 以告不孝者 - Means 'to warn the unrighteous,' indicating a moral lesson.
  • 天后朝 - Refers to the reign of Empress Wu Zetian, a female emperor of the Tang Dynasty.
  • 工人安金藏保中宗不反 - Refers to the historical figure An Jinzang, who protected Emperor Zhongzong from rebellion.
  • 自持刀开腹明之 - Means 'held a knife to his own abdomen to prove it,' indicating a dramatic act of self-sacrifice.
  • 五脏坠地 - Means 'five organs fell to the ground,' indicating severe injury.
  • 经夕复生 - Means 'revived after a night,' indicating miraculous recovery.
  • 玄宗即位，追封代国公 - Refers to the reign of Emperor Xuanzong, who posthumously honored An Jinzang as Duke of Dai.
  • 娥简狄氏 - Refers to the mythological figure E Jianti, the mother of Houji (Posterior Ji).
  • 吞鸟卵而生后稷 - Means 'swallowed an egg and gave birth to Houji,' indicating a miraculous conception.
  • 东汉陆续 - Refers to the historical figure Lu Su, a figure from the Eastern Han Dynasty.
  • 岁饥 - Means 'year of famine,' indicating a time of scarcity.
  • 太守施贫者食 - Means 'the governor distributed food to the poor,' indicating charitable action.
  • 既毕 - Means 'after finishing,' indicating the completion of the distribution.
  • 凡赐六百人 - Means 'all who received were six hundred people,' indicating the scale of the distribution.
  • 言其姓名，无一参差 - Means 'named their names, none were wrong,' indicating accuracy in record-keeping.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 韶阳 - A place name, likely referring to a region or city in ancient China.
  • 羿 - A mythological archer and hero from Chinese mythology.
  • 仙药 - Refers to elixirs of immortality, a common theme in Chinese mythology.
  • 《山海经》 - An ancient Chinese text containing mythological and geographical descriptions.
  • 大耳国 - A mythical country described in the *Shanhaijing*.
  • 贾耽 - A historical figure who served as the military governor of Huazhou during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 酸枣县 - A county in ancient China.
  • 俚妇 - Refers to a woman from a rural or lower-class background.
  • 事姑不敬 - Refers to the Confucian virtue of filial piety, specifically the respect owed to one's mother-in-law.
  • 天后朝 - Refers to the reign of Empress Wu Zetian, a female emperor of the Tang Dynasty.
  • 安金藏 - A historical figure who protected Emperor Zhongzong from rebellion.
  • 中宗 - Emperor Zhongzong of Tang, a ruler of the Tang Dynasty.
  • 玄宗 - Emperor Xuanzong of Tang, a ruler of the Tang Dynasty.
  • 娥简狄氏 - A mythological figure, the mother of Houji (Posterior Ji).
  • 后稷 - A mythological figure, the founder of agriculture in Chinese mythology.
  • 东汉 - The Eastern Han Dynasty, a period in Chinese history.
  • 陆续 - A historical figure from the Eastern Han Dynasty.
  • 岁饥 - Refers to a year of famine, a common theme in historical records.

[6]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 埋酵三千，以為殉葬 - A euphemistic phrase meaning 'to bury three thousand people as sacrifices for burial,' referring to the practice of burying alive servants and attendants with the deceased.
  • 手本拳，帝納后，以后伸之，遂展 - Describes the story of Empress Zhao, whose hand was originally clenched but uncurled after she was taken as a concubine.
  • 墨入木，深八分 - Literally means 'the ink penetrated the wood to a depth of eight points,' referring to the skillful writing of Wang Xizhi.
  • 睛不眩 - Means 'the eyes did not feel dizzy,' describing Wang Rong's ability to stare directly at the sun.
  • 策文到指上为三公，贵不可说 - A divination phrase meaning 'the writing on the palm reaches the fingertips, indicating the rank of Three Excellencies, a position of great honor.'
  • 广可以泛舟 - Means 'wide enough to sail a boat,' describing the grandeur of King Zhou's wine pool.
  • 无夫而孕 - Means 'conceiving without a husband,' describing the miraculous conception of Dong Fang Shuo.
  • 洒墙为公字 - Means 'spilling blood on the wall to form the character 'public' (公),' referring to the divination method used by Tao Kan.
  • 自见其耳 - Means 'seeing his own ears,' describing Liu Bei's unusual physical characteristic.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 吴王阖闾 - Refers to King Helu of Wu, a famous ruler of the State of Wu during the Spring and Autumn period.
  • 钩弋夫人 - Refers to Empress Zhao, a concubine of Emperor Wu of Han.
  • 王献之 - Refers to Wang Xizhi, a famous calligrapher of the Eastern Jin dynasty.
  • 王戎 - Refers to Wang Rong, a scholar and official of the Jin dynasty.
  • 廉颇 - Refers to Lian Po, a famous general of the State of Zhao during the Warring States period.
  • 陶侃 - Refers to Tao Kan, a general and politician of the Jin dynasty.
  • 殷纣 - Refers to King Zhou of Shang, the last ruler of the Shang dynasty, known for his tyrannical rule.
  • 张少平 - Refers to Zhang Shaoping, a historical figure whose wife conceived Dong Fang Shuo.
  • 蜀先主刘备 - Refers to Liu Bei, the founder of the Shu Han state during the Three Kingdoms period.

Proper Nouns

  • 吴王阖闾 - King Helu of Wu
  • 钩弋夫人 - Empress Zhao
  • 王献之 - Wang Xizhi
  • 王戎 - Wang Rong
  • 廉颇 - Lian Po
  • 陶侃 - Tao Kan
  • 殷纣 - King Zhou of Shang
  • 张少平 - Zhang Shaoping
  • 蜀先主刘备 - Liu Bei
  • 大历 - A reign title of Emperor Dezong of Tang
  • 河南尹 - Governor of Henan
  • 相里造 - A historical figure
  • 洛阳尉 - Magistrate of Luoyang

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 邹衍吹律，能变寒谷，生禾黍 - Refers to the legendary story of Zou Yan, a philosopher who could change the climate by playing his flute, transforming a cold valley into fertile land.
  • 京房吹律易姓，本李氏，因吹律知名，乃改京耳 - Describes Jing Fang, a scholar who changed his surname from Li to Jing after gaining fame for his musical expertise.
  • 牛哀病三月，化而为虎，遂食其虎，复化为人 - A mythological story about Niu Ai, who transformed into a tiger, ate the tiger, and then transformed back into a human.
  • 申包胥哭于秦廷，七日七夜不食，乞兵救楚 - Refers to Shen Baoxu, who wept for seven days and nights at the Qin court to plead for military aid to save the state of Chu.
  • 后周有一人，姓陈氏，二十而娶妻，妻亦齐年。至四十，儿女又各生孙，孙复生子，子复生孙，相承百年内，其子孙盈数百人 - Describes a person from the Later Zhou dynasty who married at age 20, and his wife was the same age. By the time he was 40, they had children, grandchildren, great-grandchildren, and so on, with over 500 descendants within a century.
  • 东汉灵帝时，有河内人妇食夫，河南人夫食妇 - Refers to a bizarre incident during the reign of Emperor Ling of Han where a woman from Hedong ate her husband, and a man from Henan ate his wife.
  • 灵帝时，洛阳女子生时两头四臂 - Describes a woman from Luoyang who was born with two heads and four arms during the reign of Emperor Ling of Han.
  • 魏文帝尝著《典论》云，天下无切玉刀、火浣布 - Refers to Emperor Wen of Wei, who wrote in his work *Dian Lun* that there were no such things as a knife that can cut jade or cloth that can be washed in fire.
  • 俄而外国进此二物，文帝遂毁《典论》 - Shortly after, foreign envoys presented these two items, and Emperor Wen of Wei then destroyed his work *Dian Lun*.
  • 古有女木兰者，代其父从征，身备戎装凡十三年，同伙之卒不知其是女儿 - Describes the legendary figure Mulan, who disguised herself as a man to take her father's place in the army for 13 years, and her fellow soldiers did not know she was a woman.

  • 木兰 - A legendary figure from Chinese folklore who disguised herself as a man to take her father's place in the army.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 不知何许人 - Indicates that the origin or background of the person is unknown.
  • 常坐一龟，畏日光 - Literally 'often sits on a turtle, fears sunlight,' which likely means the person has a turtle as a companion or a talisman.
  • 二千年一出头 - Means 'appears once every two thousand years,' referring to the rarity of the turtle's appearance.
  • 安坐来见龟五出头矣 - Means 'An has seen the turtle appear five times,' indicating the longevity and rarity of the turtle's appearances.
  • 符坚三年，凤凰集于东阁 - Means 'during the third year of Fu Jian's reign, a phoenix gathered at the east hall,' a symbolic event indicating good fortune or divine favor.
  • 欲赦国中，时无有知者 - Means 'wants to grant amnesty in the country, but no one knows about it,' indicating the secrecy of the plan.
  • 忽有一童儿，绯帕幕首言于市 - Means 'suddenly there was a child, covered with a red scarf, speaking in the market,' a mysterious and symbolic event.
  • 官家有赦 - Means 'the emperor has granted amnesty,' indicating the child's message about the amnesty.
  • 苍蝇立于笔端，久而飞去，化为童子 - Means 'a green fly stood on the tip of the pen, flew away, and transformed into a child,' a supernatural event.
  • 以告市人也 - Means 'to inform the people in the market,' suggesting the child's purpose in appearing.
  • 禹治水，渡浙江，风涛甚 - Means 'Yu the Great controlled the floods, crossed the Qiantang River, and the wind and waves were very strong,' describing the severity of the weather.
  • 二花蛇龙负舟而过 - Means 'two flowered snake dragons carried the boat past,' a fantastical description of the event.
  • 左右恐惧，惟禹安然无畏 - Means 'the attendants were afraid, but only Yu remained calm and fearless,' highlighting Yu's bravery and composure.
  • 王伐吴，于蜀江造战舰 - Means 'Wang Jun campaigned against Wu, built warships on the Shu River,' a historical event during the Three Kingdoms period.
  • 长二百四十步，上起走马楼 - Means '240 steps long, with a horse-riding floor on top,' describing a feature of the ship.
  • 舟船之盛，自古莫比 - Means 'the grandeur of the ships was unparalleled in ancient times,' emphasizing the scale and significance of the fleet.
  • 唐贞元中，有乞者解如海 - Means 'in the middle of the reign of Emperor Dezong of Tang, there was a beggar named Jie Ruhai,' indicating a historical period.
  • 手自臂而堕，足自胫而脱 - Means 'his hands fell from his arms, and his feet fell from his shins,' describing a physical anomaly.
  • 善击球、樗蒲戏，又善剑舞、数丹丸 - Means 'skilled at playing ball games and dice games, also skilled at sword dancing and counting pills,' indicating his abilities.
  • 挟二妻，生子数人 - Means 'with two wives and several children,' indicating his personal life.
  • 至元和末犹在，长安戏场中日集数千人观之 - Means 'until the end of the Yuanhe period, he was still alive, and in the theater in Chang'an, thousands of people gathered daily to watch him,' indicating his longevity and popularity.
  • 王樊卒，有盗开其冢 - Means 'King Fan died, and a thief opened his tomb,' indicating a grave robbery.
  • 见王樊与人樗蒲 - Means 'saw King Fan playing dice with someone,' a surprising and symbolic event.
  • 以酒赐盗者，盗者惶怖饮之 - Means 'gave wine to the thief, the thief drank it in fear,' indicating a supernatural interaction.
  • 见有人牵铜马出冢者 - Means 'saw someone pulling a copper horse out of the tomb,' a fantastical event.
  • 夜有神至城门，自言是王樊使 - Means 'at night, a spirit arrived at the city gate, said he was sent by King Fan,' indicating the spirit's identity.
  • 今有人发冢，以酒墨其唇，但至，可以验而擒之 - Means 'now there is someone who opened the tomb, and the thief's lips were marked with wine, just go there, and you can verify and capture him,' giving instructions.
  • 盗既入城，城门者乃缚诘之 - Means 'the thief entered the city, the gatekeeper bound and questioned him,' indicating the capture.
  • 如神言 - Means 'as the spirit had said,' confirming the accuracy of the spirit's instructions.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 玄宗打羯鼓 - Literal description of Emperor Xuanzong playing the jiegǔ (a type of drum).
  • 天宝初，春景甚煦，而卉物未柝 - Poetic description of the early spring season in the Tianbao era, where the weather was warm but plants had not yet blossomed.
  • 一曲未终，而花烂然 - Metaphorical expression indicating that the drumming caused the flowers to bloom suddenly.
  • 得不以我为圣耶 - Rhetorical question expressing the emperor's self-importance, asking if he is not considered a sage.
  • 刘伶好酒，常袒露不挂丝 - Colloquial expression describing Duan Ling's love for alcohol and his habit of appearing unclothed.
  • 我以天地为栋宇，屋室为衤昆衤夸 - Metaphorical statement where Duan Ling compares the world to a house and himself to someone who does not need to enter a house.
  • 何得入我衤昆衤夸中 - Rhetorical question expressing the idea that others should not intrude on his personal space.
  • 唐开元五年春，司天密奏云 - Formal statement indicating that in the fifth year of the Kaiyuan era, the court astronomer made a secret report.
  • 玄象有谪见，其灾甚重 - Formal expression indicating that there were ominous signs in the heavens, suggesting a severe disaster.
  • 内一人李蒙者，贵主家婿 - Formal statement identifying Li Meng as the son-in-law of a noble princess.
  • 每有大游宴，汝爱婿可闭留其家 - Formal instruction to the princess to keep her son-in-law at home during large banquets.
  • 昭国里 - Name of a specific area in the capital city.
  • 时大合乐，音曲远畅 - Formal description of a grand musical performance with music spreading far and wide.
  • 曲江涨水，联舟数十艘 - Formal description of the Qujiang River overflowing and several boats being connected together.
  • 蒙闻之，乃逾垣走赴 - Formal description of Li Meng climbing over a wall to join the festivities.
  • 暴风忽起，画舸平沈 - Formal description of a sudden storm causing the boats to sink.
  • 声伎、持篙楫者不知纪极 - Formal description of the chaos caused by the storm, with performers and boatmen unable to be counted.
  • 三十八人无一生者 - Formal statement indicating that all 38 people died in the disaster.

  • 见壶鬓发苍白，面色如生，两手皆拳，甲穿于手背 - Describes the condition of Bian Hu's body when the tomb was opened: his hair and beard were white, his face looked as if he were alive, his hands were clenched into fists, and his nails had penetrated his palms.
  • 安帝赐钱十万，令改葬焉 - States that Emperor An granted ten thousand coins to have Bian Hu reburied.
  • 挚虞《要注》云 - Indicates a citation from Zhi Yu's *Yaozhu* (Essential Annotations).
  • 晋武帝时，有彭权为侍中 - Refers to the time of Emperor Wu of Jin when Peng Quan served as a high-ranking official.
  • 帝问“髦头”义 - Means 'the emperor asked about the meaning of 'maotou'.
  • 权答曰：“秦时有奇怪，触山截水，无不崩溃，惟畏髦头。” - Peng Quan's response explains that 'maotou' refers to something from the Qin dynasty that could destroy mountains and rivers but was feared by the 'maotou'.
  • 帝乃令虎贲之士戴之，以卫左右 - States that Emperor Wu ordered the Tiger Guards to wear 'maotou' to protect him.
  • 河东裴章者，其父胄，曾镇荆州 - Introduces Pei Zhang, whose father Pei Zou once governed Jingzhou.
  • 门僧昙照，道行甚高，能知休咎 - Describes Men Seng Tan Zao, a monk with high spiritual attainments who could predict good and bad omens.
  • 章幼时为昙照所重，言其官班位望过于其父 - States that Pei Zhang was highly regarded by Tan Zao, who predicted that Pei Zhang would surpass his father in rank and reputation.
  • 章弱冠，父为娶李氏女 - Indicates that Pei Zhang, when he came of age, was married to a woman from the Li family.
  • 乃三十年余，章从职太原，弃其妻于洛中，过门不入，别有所挈 - Describes how Pei Zhang, after thirty years, left his wife in Luoyang and moved to Taiyuan without visiting her, taking another woman with him.
  • 李氏自感其薄命，常褐衣ヮ髻，读佛书，蔬食 - States that Pei Zhang's first wife felt her fate was unfortunate, so she dressed simply, wore her hair in a simple style, read Buddhist scriptures, and ate a vegetarian diet.
  • 又十年，严经自荆州移镇太原，昙照随之 - Indicates that after another ten years, Yan Jing moved from Jingzhou to govern Taiyuan, and Tan Zao followed him.
  • 章因见昙照叙旧 - States that Pei Zhang met Tan Zao and reminisced about the past.
  • 照惊噫久之，谓之曰：“贫道五十年前常谓郎君必贵，今削尽，何也” - Tan Zao was surprised and exclaimed for a long time, saying, 'Fifty years ago, I always said that you would become a nobleman. Now it seems all is lost, why is this?'
  • 章自以薄妻之事启之 - Pei Zhang revealed to Tan Zao that he had abandoned his first wife for another woman.
  • 照曰：“夫人生魂诉上帝，以罪处君。” - Tan Zao explained that Pei Zhang's first wife had complained to the heavens about him, and he was punished for his sins.
  • 后旬日，为其下以刀划腹于浴斛，五脏堕地遂死 - States that ten days later, Pei Zhang was killed by his subordinates who cut open his abdomen in a bathtub, causing his internal organs to fall out and leading to his death.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 漢光武愛惜士卒，每欲發一兵，頭須悉白 - Describes Emperor Guangwu's concern for his soldiers, leading to his hair and beard turning white. The phrase '每欲發一兵' can be interpreted as 'every time he wanted to send out a single soldier,' indicating his reluctance to engage in conflict.
  • 伏日對武帝拔劍割肉，帝令自責 - Describes Eastern朔's act of cutting meat on the伏 day and Emperor Wu's command for him to blame himself. The phrase '帝令自責' can be interpreted as 'the emperor ordered him to blame himself,' indicating a gesture of humility.
  • 秦穆公亡善馬，岐山野人共得而食之 - Describes Emperor Mu of Qin's loss of his favorite horse and the people of Qishan eating it. The phrase '共得而食之' can be interpreted as 'they shared and ate it,' indicating a communal act.
  • 吾聞食馬肉不飲酒，傷人 - Describes the belief that eating horse meat without drinking wine harms people. The phrase '吾聞' can be interpreted as 'I have heard,' indicating a traditional belief.
  • 而三百人爭死以報恩 - Describes three hundred men fighting to the death to repay the favor. The phrase '爭死以報恩' can be interpreted as 'they fought to the death to repay the favor,' indicating their loyalty and gratitude.
  • 衛為性通恕，常自戒曰 - Describes Wèi 's broad-minded and forgiving nature and his self-discipline.

  • 忽有少年過門求食 - Suddenly, a young man passed by the door asking for food.
  • 待之甚恭 - Treated him with great respect.
  • 時往縣，見門阃有血，即可登山避難 - Whenever you go to the county, if you see blood at the threshold, climb the mountain to avoid disaster.
  • 日往之門 - Go to the door every day.
  • 吏問其狀 - The official asked about the situation.
  • 戲以雞血涂門阃 - Played a trick by painting the threshold with chicken blood.
  • 傅奕常不信佛法 - Fu Yi often did not believe in Buddhism.
  • 高祖時有西國胡僧 - During the reign of Emperor Gaozu, there was a foreign monk from the Western countries.
  • 此胡法不足信 - This foreign method is not trustworthy.
  • 若火能燒臣，即為聖者 - If the fire can burn me, then he is a saint.
  • 立胡僧於殿西，奕於殿东 - The monk was placed on the west side of the hall, and Fu Yi on the east side.
  • 乃令胡僧作法 - Then ordered the monk to perform his magic.
  • 乾，元亨利贞 - Qian, yuan heng li zhen (the opening hexagram of the *I Ching*).
  • 邪不干正 - Evil cannot harm righteousness.
  • 晋孝武欲为晋陵公主求婿 - Emperor Xiaowu of Jin wanted to find a husband for Princess Jinling.
  • 得及刘真长、王子敬便足 - If he could match Liu Zhenzhang and Wang Zijing, that would be enough.
  • 谢琨不及刘真长，不减王子敬 - Xie Kun is not as good as Liu Zhenzhang, but not inferior to Wang Zijing.
  • 元帝初渡江 - During the early years of Emperor Yuan's crossing of the river.
  • 国内常乏 - The country was often short of resources.
  • 朝士每烹猪，以项肉一斤尤脆美 - Officials often cooked pork, and the neck meat was especially tender and delicious.
  • 禁脔 - A forbidden delicacy.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 傅奕 - A historical figure, a scholar and critic of Buddhism.
  • 高祖 - Refers to Emperor Gaozu of Tang, the founder of the Tang Dynasty.
  • 胡僧 - Refers to a foreign monk, likely from Central Asia.
  • 晋孝武 - Emperor Xiaowu of Jin, a ruler of the Eastern Jin Dynasty.
  • 晋陵公主 - Princess Jinling, a member of the Jin royal family.
  • 刘真长 - Liu Zhenzhang, a prominent official and scholar of the Jin Dynasty.
  • 王子敬 - Wang Zijing, a famous calligrapher and official of the Jin Dynasty.
  • 谢琨 - Xie Kun, a historical figure, likely a relative or associate of the Xie family.
  • 元帝 - Emperor Yuan of Jin, a ruler of the Eastern Jin Dynasty.
  • 禁脔 - A term for a delicacy that was off-limits or reserved for special occasions.

Proper Nouns

  • 傅奕 - A historical figure, a scholar and critic of Buddhism.
  • 高祖 - Refers to Emperor Gaozu of Tang.
  • 胡僧 - Refers to a foreign monk.
  • 晋孝武 - Emperor Xiaowu of Jin.
  • 晋陵公主 - Princess Jinling.
  • 刘真长 - Liu Zhenzhang.
  • 王子敬 - Wang Zijing.
  • 谢琨 - Xie Kun.
  • 元帝 - Emperor Yuan of Jin.

  • 会稽 - A region in present-day Zhejiang Province.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 喟然有志焉 - Expresses a sigh of deep emotion or reflection.
  • 以問御者 - Means 'to ask the driver.'
  • 何馬也 - A straightforward question meaning 'What kind of horse is this?'
  • 以公家畜 - Refers to a horse belonging to the state or public.
  • 罷而不用 - Means 'tired and not used,' indicating the horse is old and no longer in service.
  • 少盡其力 - Means 'when young, it has expended its strength.'
  • 老棄其身 - Means 'when old, it is discarded.'
  • 仁者不為 - A statement of moral principle, meaning 'a benevolent person would not do this.'
  • 遂束物以贖之 - Means 'he then tied up some goods to redeem it.'
  • 知所歸矣 - Means 'knew where to return.'
  • 卒母哭曰 - Means 'the mother of the deceased cried and said.'
  • 往年吳起吮其父疽 - Means 'last year Wu Qi sucked his father's wound.'
  • 父不旋踵而死 - Means 'his father died without turning his foot.'
  • 塞翁失馬 - A well-known Chinese idiom meaning 'the old man lost his horse,' often used to illustrate the principle of good and bad luck being relative.
  • 鄉人皆唁 - Means 'all the villagers came to mourn.'
  • 未必不為福 - Means 'it may not be a misfortune.'
  • 明年，引群馬至 - Means 'the next year, a group of horses arrived.'
  • 人復賀 - Means 'people came to congratulate.'
  • 未必不為禍 - Means 'it may not be a blessing.'
  • 墜折四肢 - Means 'falling and breaking limbs.'
  • 闔境並不言祜字 - Means 'throughout the territory, no one mentioned the character 祜.'
  • 同音，亦改諱之 - Means 'even if it had the same sound, they also changed it.'
  • 襄陽百姓於峴山立墮淚碑 - Means 'the people of Xiangyang erected a weeping stone on Xianshan.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 齊田子方 - Tian Zifang, a historical figure from the state of Qi.
  • 吳起 - Wu Qi, a famous general and statesman from the Warring States period.
  • 塞翁 - The old man from the idiom '塞翁失马' (the old man lost his horse).
  • 羊祜 - Yang Hu, a high-ranking official and military leader from the Three Kingdoms period.
  • 襄陽 - Xiangyang, a city in Hubei Province, China.
  • 峴山 - Xianshan, a mountain near Xiangyang.

  • 峴山 - Xianshan.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 因畅为河南尹 - Formal statement indicating that someone named 'Chang' became the governor of Henan.
  • 祷祠无应 - Means 'prayers and sacrifices were unanswered,' referring to the failure of religious rituals to bring rain.
  • 收葬傍城客死骸骨百余具 - Means 'to collect and bury over a hundred corpses of travelers who died near the city.' The term '客死' (died as a guest) is used to describe travelers who died away from home.
  • 遽降大雨，年谷丰稔 - Means 'it suddenly rained heavily, and the crops were abundant.'
  • 偶逢骏马，爱之，其主所惜也 - Means 'chanced upon a fine horse, loved it, which its owner cherished.'
  • 余有美妾可换，唯君所选 - Means 'I have a beautiful concubine who can be exchanged, you may choose her.'
  • 后因猎，献于文帝 - Means 'later, during a hunt, he presented it to Emperor Wen.'
  • 《武陵记》曰 - Refers to the *Wuling Ji* (Records of Wu Ling), an ancient text.
  • 后汉马融勤学，梦见一林，花如绣锦，梦中摘此花食之 - Means 'During the Eastern Han dynasty, Ma Rong, who was diligent in his studies, dreamed of a forest where the flowers were like embroidered brocade, and in the dream, he picked and ate these flowers.'
  • 及寤，见天下文词，无所不知 - Means 'Upon waking, he found that he knew all the literary works in the world.'
  • 崔豹《古今注》 - Refers to Cui Bao's *Gujin Zhu* (Notes on Ancient and Modern Times), an ancient text.
  • 齐王后怨死，尸化为蝉，遂登庭树，ィ唳而鸣 - Means 'The queen of Qi died in resentment, and her body transformed into a cicada, which then climbed onto a courtyard tree and chirped.'
  • 后王悔恨，闻蝉即悲叹 - Means 'Later, the king regretted and would weep upon hearing the cicadas.'
  • 陈宣帝时，扬州人严泰江行逢渔舟，问之，云：“有龟五十头。” - Means 'During the reign of Emperor Xuan of Chen, a man named Yan Tai from Yangzhou encountered a fishing boat while traveling on the river and asked about it; the fisherman said, 'There are fifty turtles.'
  • 泰用钱五百赎放之 - Means 'Yan Tai paid 500 coins to redeem and release them.'
  • 行数十步，渔舟乃覆 - Means 'After walking several dozen steps, the fishing boat capsized.'
  • 其夕，有乌衣五十人扣泰门，谓其父母曰：“贤郎附钱五百，可领之。” - Means 'That night, fifty people dressed in black robes knocked on Yan Tai's door and told his parents, 'Your son has brought 500 coins; you may collect them.'
  • 缗皆濡湿 - Means 'The coins were all wet.'
  • 父母虽受钱，不知其由 - Means 'His parents accepted the coins but did not know the reason.'
  • 泰归，问焉，乃赎龟之异 - Means 'Yan Tai returned home and asked about it, and it turned out to be a strange event related to redeeming the turtles.'
  • 因以其居为寺 - Means 'He then built a temple on the site.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 后魏 - Refers to the Northern Wei dynasty, a significant period in Chinese history.
  • 曹彰 - A historical figure known for his bravery and leadership.
  • 后汉 - Refers to the Eastern Han dynasty, another significant period in Chinese history.
  • 马融 - A famous scholar and literary figure during the Eastern Han dynasty.
  • 绣囊 - A nickname given to Ma Rong due to his exceptional knowledge.
  • 崔豹《古今注》 - An ancient text that records various historical and cultural facts.
  • 齐王 - Refers to the king of the state of Qi during the Warring States period.
  • 陈宣帝 - Refers to Emperor Xuan of Chen, a ruler during the Southern Dynasties period.
  • 法严寺 - A temple built on the site where a miraculous event occurred.

  • 绣囊 - A nickname for Ma Rong.
  • 崔豹《古今注》 - An ancient text.
  • 齐王 - The king of the state of Qi.

  • 恶之 - Means 'to consider it evil' or 'to regard it as ominous.'
  • 作色 - Means 'to change color' or 'to become angry.'
  • 言讫 - Means 'after speaking.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 晋王导 - Refers to Wang Dao, a prominent politician and military leader during the Eastern Jin dynasty.
  • 汉景帝 - Refers to Emperor Jing of Han, the sixth emperor of the Han dynasty.
  • 蒋山侯 - A title given to a noble or a deity.
  • 桓帝 - Refers to Emperor Huan of Han, the tenth emperor of the Eastern Han dynasty.

  • 刺史 - An official title in the Han dynasty, similar to a governor.
  • 吕氏春秋 - A philosophical and historical text compiled by Lü Buwei, a chancellor of the Qin state during the Warring States period.

  • 潜龙 - A metaphorical term often used to describe a person of great potential who is not yet recognized.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 梁武帝大清三年 - Specific date in the reign of Emperor Wu of Liang, using the era name 'Dachen'.
  • 侯景反，圍台城 - Refers to the rebellion of Hou Jing, who besieged Tai Cheng, the capital of the Liang dynasty.
  • 遠近不通 - Concise phrase meaning 'communication was cut off', indicating the isolation caused by the siege.
  • 簡文與太子大器為計 - Means 'Emperor Jianwen and Crown Prince Daqi devised a plan'.
  • 告急于外 - Means 'to send an urgent message to the outside world'.
  • 侯景謀臣謂景曰 - Means 'Hou Jing's advisors told Hou Jing'.
  • 此必厭勝術 - Refers to the idea that the kite might be a form of magical or superstitious ritual.
  • 不然即事达人 - Means 'otherwise, it is a clever device to reach people'.
  • 令左右射之 - Means 'ordered his attendants to shoot it down'.
  • 及墮，皆化為禽鳥飛去 - Means 'when it fell, it transformed into birds and flew away'.
  • 不知所在 - Means 'no one knew where they went'.
  • 《京房列傳》曰 - Refers to the biography of Jing Fang in the Book of Han.
  • 房臨刑之时 - Means 'Jing Fang was about to be executed'.
  • 謂人曰 - Means 'told someone'.
  • 吾死之后，客星入天井 - Refers to a prediction that after his death, a guest star would enter the well (天井), a metaphor for his tomb.
  • 舉朝皆哀之 - Means 'the entire court mourned him'.
  • 王充《論衡》云 - Refers to Wang Chong's Lunheng (Balanced Discourse).
  • 漢李子長為政 - Refers to Li Zichang, a figure in Wang Chong's text.
  • 欲知囚情 - Means 'wanted to know the truth of the prisoner's situation'.
  • 以桐木刻為囚象 - Means 'carved a wooden figure of the prisoner'.
  • 致木人拷訊之 - Means 'placed the wooden figure under interrogation'.
  • 若正罪則木人不動 - Means 'if the prisoner was guilty, the wooden figure would not move'.
  • 如冤枉則木人搖其頭 - Means 'if the prisoner was innocent, the wooden figure would shake its head'.
  • 精感立政 - Means 'the power of spirit and emotion can establish justice'.
  • 王子年《拾遺記》曰 - Refers to Wang Zini's Shiyiji (Record of Lost and Found).
  • 燕昭王时 - Refers to the time of King Zhao of Yan.
  • 波弋国人贡茶芜之香 - Refers to the gift of a fragrant tea from the people of Boyi.
  • 若焚，著衣而弥月不绝 - Means 'if burned, the fragrance would linger on clothing for a month'.
  • 过地则土石皆香 - Means 'if passed over the ground, the earth and stones would become fragrant'.
  • 经朽木与腐草则皆荣秀 - Means 'if passed over decaying wood and rotten grass, they would become lush and beautiful'.
  • 用薰枯骨则肌肉再生 - Means 'if used to fumigate dry bones, flesh would regenerate'.
  • 后汉明帝杨后 - Refers to Empress Yang of the Eastern Han dynasty.
  • 花面美色 - Means 'beautiful and colorful face'.
  • 有颠狂病 - Means 'suffered from a form of mental illness'.
  • 发则杀人 - Means 'when she became violent, she would kill people'.
  • 唯内傅孟召为文哀怨 - Refers to the tutor Meng Zhao, who wrote mournful and sad texts.
  • 后每读之，颠狂辄醒 - Means 'Empress Yang would read them, and her madness would subside'.
  • 时人语曰 - Means 'people at the time said'.
  • 孟召文，差颠狂 - Means 'Meng Zhao's writing was a remedy for her madness'.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 梁武帝大清三年 - The year 549 CE during the reign of Emperor Wu of Liang.
  • 侯景 - A general who rebelled against the Liang dynasty, leading to the fall of the capital.
  • 台城 - The imperial palace and capital of the Liang dynasty.
  • 简文 - Emperor Jianwen of Liang, the ruler of the Liang dynasty.
  • 太子大器 - Crown Prince Daqi, the heir apparent to the Liang throne.
  • 《京房列传》 - A biography of Jing Fang, a Han dynasty astrologer and scholar.
  • 王充《论衡》 - A work by Wang Chong, a Han dynasty philosopher and critic.
  • 王子年《拾遗记》 - A collection of historical and mythological stories by Wang Zini.
  • 燕昭王 - King Zhao of Yan, a ruler of the Warring States period.
  • 后汉明帝杨后 - Empress Yang of the Eastern Han dynasty, known for her beauty and mental illness.

Proper Nouns

  • 梁武帝 - Emperor Wu of Liang.
  • 侯景 - General Hou Jing.
  • 简文 - Emperor Jianwen of Liang.
  • 太子大器 - Crown Prince Daqi.
  • 京房 - Jing Fang, a Han dynasty astrologer.
  • 王充 - Wang Chong, a Han dynasty philosopher.
  • 王子年 - Wang Zini, a writer of the Six Dynasties period.
  • 燕昭王 - King Zhao of Yan.
  • 后汉明帝杨后 - Empress Yang of the Eastern Han dynasty.
  • 孟召 - Meng Zhao, a tutor to Empress Yang.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 玉箱記 - Title of a historical or anecdotal text, possibly referring to a collection of stories or records.
  • 西征過山而渴 - Literal description of Liu Ziguang's journey westward, crossing a mountain and becoming thirsty.
  • 石人不答 - Literal description of the stone figure not responding.
  • 拔劍斬石人 - Literal description of Liu Ziguang drawing his sword and cutting the stone figure.
  • 窮山水出 - Literal description of the mountain suddenly producing water.
  • 同是殷夫人四月二日孪生 - Literal description of the twins being born on the same day in April to Lady Yīn.
  • 又以四月二日同刑于都市 - Literal description of the twins being executed on the same day in April in the capital city.
  • 悉生嘉禾 - Literal description of all the land producing fine rice.
  • 北斗与三台并流 - Literal description of the Big Dipper and the Three Stars (a constellation) flowing together.
  • 不知其所 - Literal description of not knowing where they went.
  • 两主星复见 - Literal description of the two main stars reappearing.
  • 明旦往伐之 - Literal description of going to chop it down the next day.
  • 其夕，汹汹然，波浪振惊 - Literal description of that night, the waves were turbulent and alarming.
  • 查浮，遂移去 - Literal description of the log floating away and moving off.
  • 不为江中之患 - Literal description of no longer being a problem in the river.
  • 立碑，以诵祚之德也 - Literal description of setting up a stele to praise Ge Zuó's virtue.
  • 悦书吏 - Literal description of the daughter liking a clerk.
  • 乃密使侍婢取吏食余残水，饮之 - Literal description of secretly sending a servant to fetch the clerk's leftover water and drink it.
  • 遂有孕 - Literal description of becoming pregnant.
  • 及岁 - Literal description of by the time she was a year old.
  • 太守使抱出门 - Literal description of the governor having the child taken out of the door.
  • 儿匍匐入吏怀 - Literal description of the child crawling into the clerk's arms.
  • 吏推之仆地 - Literal description of the clerk pushing the child down to the ground.
  • 化为水 - Literal description of turning into water.
  • 具省前事 - Literal description of fully understanding the previous events.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 齐王奂 - Refers to King Huàn of Qi, a historical figure.
  • 梁武太清元年 - Refers to the first year of the Taichang era during the reign of Emperor Wu of Liang.
  • 岳阳郡 - Refers to Yueyang, a historical region.
  • 周厉王 - Refers to King Lì of Zhou, a historical figure.
  • 北斗与三台 - Refers to the Big Dipper and the Three Stars, constellations in Chinese astronomy.
  • 吴时 - Refers to the time of the state of Wu.
  • 衡阳 - Refers to Héngyáng, a historical region.
  • 干宝《搜神记》 - Refers to Gan Bǎo's *Search for the Supernatural*, a collection of folktales and supernatural stories.
  • 零陵 - Refers to Línglíng, a historical region.

Proper Nouns

  • 刘子光 - Liu Ziguang, a historical figure.
  • 融、琛 - The names of King Huàn's two sons.
  • 王保 - Wang Bao, a historical figure.
  • 葛祚 - Ge Zuó, a historical figure.
  • 干宝 - Gan Bǎo, author of *Search for the Supernatural*.
  • 史满 - Shǐ Mǎn, the governor of Línglíng.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 拜司空 - Formal title-granting ceremony for the position of Sikong, one of the Three Excellencies in the Han dynasty.
  • 夜有人扣門請見 - Formal expression meaning 'someone knocked on the door at night to request an audience.'
  • 白虎使者 - A mysterious figure or supernatural being, possibly a guardian or messenger.
  • 皆衣白衣 - All dressed in white robes, indicating a ritual or supernatural appearance.
  • 懷中探一物，內懿手中 - From his chest, he took out an object and placed it in the hand of Sima Yi, describing a mysterious transfer.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 蚩尤是古之帝者 - Phrase stating that Chiyou was an ancient emperor.
  • 兄弟八十一人 - Literal phrase meaning 'eighty-one brothers,' referring to the large number of Chiyou's followers.
  • 铜头铁额 - Metaphorical phrase meaning 'copper head and iron forehead,' describing the physical appearance of Chiyou and his followers.

  • 为黄帝所灭也 - Phrase meaning 'were ultimately defeated by the Yellow Emperor,' indicating the historical outcome.
  • 《搜神记》曰 - Introductory phrase indicating that what follows is a quote from the *Shenxian Ji* (《搜神记》), a collection of supernatural stories.

  • 遂投身而死 - Phrase meaning 'she threw herself into the grave and died,' describing the wife's sacrifice.
  • 有赤气如匹绛 - Phrase meaning 'there was a red vapor like a piece of red silk,' describing a supernatural phenomenon.
  • 时人谓之“蚩尤旗” - Phrase meaning 'people at that time called it the 'Chiyou Flag,' indicating a cultural interpretation of the phenomenon.
  • 《西京杂记》 - Title of a historical text, *Xijing Zaji* (《西京杂记》), which contains various anecdotes and historical notes.
  • 弘成子少时 - Phrase meaning 'when Hong Chengzi was young,' introducing a character and time period.
  • 好学 - Phrase meaning 'loved learning,' describing a character trait.
  • 尝有人过门授一文石 - Phrase meaning 'once a person passed by the door and gave him a piece of jade,' describing a mysterious event.
  • 吞之遂明悟 - Phrase meaning 'after swallowing it, he became enlightened,' indicating a transformative experience.
  • 为天下通儒 - Phrase meaning 'became a renowned scholar,' describing the character's achievement.
  • 五鹿充宗受学成子 - Phrase meaning 'Wu Lu Chongzong received teachings from Chengzi,' introducing a new character and relationship.
  • 成子一日病 - Phrase meaning 'one day Chengzi fell ill,' indicating a change in the narrative.
  • 乃吐此石 - Phrase meaning 'he then spat out the stone,' describing a physical action.

  • 又为名儒 - Phrase meaning 'and also became a famous scholar,' describing the new character's achievement.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 微時 - Classical term meaning 'in humble or lowly times,' used to describe someone's early or impoverished life.
  • 丁父艰 - Term from ancient Chinese mourning practices, meaning 'to suffer the loss of one's father' (i.e., to be in mourning for one's father).
  • 位极人臣 - Phrase meaning 'to attain the highest rank among ministers,' indicating the pinnacle of political power.
  • 当世出二千石 - Term referring to the rank of a *shangshu* (a high-ranking official, often a governor or magistrate), with '二千石' being a salary rank used to denote official status.
  • 以忠贞见黜 - Phrase meaning 'was dismissed from office due to his integrity,' indicating that the person was removed from position because of his moral uprightness.
  • 帑藏空虚 - Phrase meaning 'the treasury is empty' or 'the state coffers are depleted.'
  • 饮鸩而卒 - Term meaning 'drank poison and died,' often used metaphorically to describe someone who chose death over dishonor.
  • 门人冤之 - Phrase meaning 'his disciples mourned him' or 'his followers felt wronged by his death.'
  • 天子嘉之 - Phrase meaning 'the emperor praised him' or 'the Son of Heaven commended him.'
  • 大鸟翼一丈三尺 - Literal description of a large bird with a wingspan of thirteen feet, used to symbolize a supernatural or divine omen.
  • 集于柩前 - Phrase meaning 'gathered before the coffin,' indicating the bird's presence at the funeral.
  • 低头垂泪 - Phrase meaning 'lowered its head and wept,' suggesting emotional or symbolic mourning.
  • 隐居华山 - Phrase meaning 'to live in seclusion on Mount Hua,' indicating a life of reclusion and spiritual cultivation.
  • 禽兽日游目前 - Phrase meaning 'wild animals roamed daily before him,' suggesting a harmonious relationship with nature.
  • 有如家驯 - Phrase meaning 'as if tamed at home,' indicating that the animals were accustomed to him.
  • 不食菜心 - Phrase meaning 'did not eat the center of the vegetables,' suggesting a practice of avoiding destruction of the plant's reproductive or vital parts.
  • 日料三十颗 - Phrase meaning 'daily ration of thirty fruits,' indicating a self-imposed limit on consumption.
  • 异日如戒 - Phrase meaning 'later, it followed the rule' or 'later, it complied with the limit,' showing that the birds respected the hermit’s decree.
  • 悲鸣累日而去 - Phrase meaning 'mourned for many days and then departed,' suggesting a farewell or emotional send-off.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 晋陶侃 - Refers to Tao Kan (陶侃), a prominent general and statesman of the Eastern Jin dynasty, known for his integrity and contributions to the state.
  • 丁父艰 - Term from Confucian mourning rituals, indicating the period of mourning for one’s father, during which one would not take up official duties.
  • 位极人臣 - Phrase used to describe a minister who has reached the highest rank in the imperial court.
  • 二千石 - Rank in the Han and later dynasties, referring to officials who received a salary of 2000 *dan* (a unit of grain), often used for governors or high-ranking officials.
  • 汉太尉杨震 - Refers to Yang Zhen (杨震), a Han dynasty official known for his integrity and moral courage, who was dismissed from office for refusing to comply with corrupt officials.
  • 樊丰 - Refers to Fan Feng, a corrupt official under Emperor Shang of Han, known for his power and influence.
  • 帑藏空虚 - Refers to the depletion of the imperial treasury, a common issue during dynastic decline.
  • 饮鸩而卒 - Metaphorical expression for dying due to moral integrity, often used in classical Chinese to describe the death of a virtuous person.
  • 门人冤之 - Refers to the disciples or followers of Yang Zhen who felt unjustly wronged by his death.
  • 天子嘉之 - Refers to the emperor praising or honoring Yang Zhen posthumously.
  • 大鸟翼一丈三尺 - Likely refers to a mythical or symbolic bird, possibly a phoenix or a divine omen, indicating the supernatural recognition of Yang Zhen’s virtue.
  • 《会稽记》 - Classical geographical and historical text about the region of Kuaiji (modern-day Shaoxing in Zhejiang Province), compiled by Gao Yan during the Eastern Jin dynasty.
  • 上虞兰室山 - Refers to Lan Shi Mountain in Shangyu, a district of Kuaiji, where the legendary Daoist figure Ge Xuan (葛玄) is said to have lived in seclusion.
  • 隐几化为鹿 - Daoist legend where a table (隐几) used by a hermit transforms into a deer, symbolizing the supernatural powers of Daoist practitioners.
  • 鹿鸣，即县令有罪 - Belief that the sound of a deer (the *Lu* or *Lu Ming*, a classical poem) is an omen of a corrupt or unjust county magistrate.
  • 司马郊，字子都 - Refers to Sima Jiao (司马郊), a hermit who lived in seclusion on Mount Hua for over fifty years. His name and title are not widely known in historical records, suggesting this may be a fictional or semi-legendary figure.
  • 华山 - One of the Five Great Mountains of China, known for its spiritual and Daoist significance.
  • 禽兽日游目前 - Daoist or Buddhist concept of harmony with nature, suggesting that a sage or hermit can live in peace with the natural world.
  • 不食菜心 - Practice of refraining from eating the center of vegetables, symbolizing respect for life and the natural order.
  • 日料三十颗 - Self-imposed limit, suggesting ascetic discipline and moderation in consumption.
  • 异日如戒 - Refers to the birds obeying the hermit’s rule, indicating a supernatural or spiritual bond.
  • 悲鸣累日而去 - Farewell of the natural world to the hermit upon his death, symbolizing the emotional and spiritual impact of his life.

  • 用鼠须笔蚕茧纸 - Refers to using mouse whisker brushes and silk cocoon paper, which were high-quality materials for writing.
  • 平生之札，最为得意 - Means 'the best work of his life,' emphasizing the significance of the *Lanting* (Orchid Pavilion) inscription.
  • 爵赏之外，别费亿万 - Means 'beyond the titles and rewards, additional costs in the millions,' indicating the immense value placed on the *Lanting* (Orchid Pavilion) inscription.
  • 以《兰亭》殉吾，孝也 - Means 'to bury the *Lanting* (Orchid Pavilion) with me, is filial piety,' expressing the emperor's wish to be buried with the *Lanting* (Orchid Pavilion) inscription.
  • 得葡萄、涂林、安石榴 - Refers to obtaining grapes, *tulin* (likely a type of fruit), and pomegranates, which were introduced to China from the West.
  • 害人者低头 - Means 'the one that harms people will lower its head,' indicating that the more aggressive tiger will submit to authority.
  • 吾道东矣 - Means 'my way has spread to the east,' suggesting that Vajrayana Buddhism (or another form of Buddhism) has spread to the east.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 晋索报兄之仇 - Elliptical phrase; Jìn Suǒ Mǐ avenged his brother's death by personally killing forty people.
  • 手杀四十人 - Literally means 'killed forty people with his hands,' emphasizing personal and direct action.
  • 归见其妻织，乃焚机而出 - Ambiguous phrase; on returning home, he saw his wife weaving, and then burned the loom and left.
  • 吾为相食禄，今尔夺百姓之利，使民安归哉 - Elliptical phrase; I receive a salary as chancellor, but now you are taking away the people's benefits, so where will the people return to?
  • 隐药肆，卖价无二 - Elliptical phrase; He lived incognito in a pharmacy, selling goods at a consistent price.
  • 君何若康伯无二价也 - Elliptical phrase; How can you be like Kang Bo with an unchanged price?
  • 东明走，弯弓射水，鱼鳖浮出而为梁，以渡东明 - Elliptical phrase; Dongming ran away, drew his bow and shot at the water, causing fish and turtles to float out and form a bridge, allowing Dongming to cross.
  • 魏武帝尝居铜雀台，及终，令妓乐登台望西陵而歌舞 - Elliptical phrase; Emperor Wei Wu once stayed at the Copper Sparrow Tower, and upon his death, he ordered musicians and dancers to climb the tower and look toward the Western Tomb while dancing and singing.
  • 郑子华之弟子臧，好聚鹬毛为冠，郑伯闻而恶甚，使盗诱杀之 - Elliptical phrase; Zeng Zha, a disciple of Zheng Zihua, liked to collect heron feathers to make hats. Zheng Bo heard about this and detested it greatly, so he sent a thief to lure and kill him.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 宿于逆旅 - Staying at an inn; a common expression for temporary lodging.
  • 客寝甚甘 - The guest slept very soundly; '甘' (sweet) is used metaphorically to describe a pleasant sleep.
  • 殆非就国者也 - Probably not someone who is going to take office; '殆' (probably) and '就国' (take office) are key components.
  • 蹶起即路 - Jumped up and headed out immediately; '蹶起' (jumped up) and '即路' (headed out) are vivid expressions.
  • 俄有追者至 - Soon someone came to chase him; '俄' (soon) and '追者' (chaser) are important.
  • 以其出关 - Because he had left the pass; '以其' (because) and '出关' (left the pass) are key phrases.
  • 齐桓公与管仲谋伐莒 - Duke Huan of Qi and Guan Zhong planned to attack Ju; '谋伐' (planned to attack) is a key phrase.
  • 国人知之 - The people knew about it; '国人' (the people) and '知之' (knew about it) are important.
  • 意者左右有圣人乎 - Perhaps there is a sage among your attendants?; '意者' (perhaps) and '圣人' (sage) are key terms.

  • 猛厉忠实，兵革之色 - The expression of sternness and loyalty, like the sound of war; '猛厉忠实' (sternness and loyalty) and '兵革之色' (expression of war) are vivid metaphors.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 太康畋于洛之表 - 太康是传说中的中国皇帝，畋于洛之表意为在洛阳郊外打猎。
  • 十日不返 - 意为十天没有返回，表示长时间的缺席。
  • 辇其母以从 - 辇意为用车载，以从意为跟随，表示他的弟弟们用车载着母亲去跟随他。
  • 终失国 - 终意为最终，失国意为失去国家，表示他的行为最终导致国家的丧失。
  • 汉王欲以赵王如意易太子 - 汉王指刘邦，赵王如意是赵王，易太子意为更换太子，表示汉王想用赵王如意替换太子。
  • 吕后问计于张良 - 吕后是刘邦的皇后，问计于张良意为向张良询问计策，表示吕后寻求张良的建议。
  • 南山有四皓 - 南山指山名，四皓是四位隐士，表示南山有四位隐士。
  • 卑辞延之 - 卑辞意为谦卑的言辞，延之意为邀请，表示用谦卑的言辞邀请他们。
  • 若四老人到，扶太子，一助也 - 若四老人到意为如果四位老人到来，扶太子意为支持太子，表示四位老人的到来将支持太子。
  • 高帝曰：“吾得天下，不到。” - 高帝是刘邦的另一个称呼，吾得天下，不到意为我得到天下，但没有来，表示刘邦的缺席。
  • 今从吾儿游，何也 - 今从吾儿游意为现在跟随我的儿子，何也意为为什么，表示询问他们为何跟随。
  • 陛下侮慢，臣等耻来 - 陛下是皇帝的尊称，侮慢意为轻视，臣等耻来意为我们感到羞耻而来，表示他们对皇帝的轻视感到羞耻。
  • 今太子贤明，臣故佐之 - 今太子贤明意为现在的太子贤明，臣故佐之意为我们因此支持他，表示他们支持太子。
  • 高祖谓戚夫人曰：“羽翼已成，难动摇矣。” - 高祖是刘邦的另一个称呼，羽翼已成，难动摇矣意为翅膀已经长成，难以动摇，表示太子的地位稳固。
  • 张嘉为相州刺史，至都，询故事 - 张嘉是人名，为相州刺史意为担任相州刺史，至都，询故事意为到达都城，询问旧事，表示他询问历史惯例。
  • 皆云前后太守多不生出郡城 - 皆云意为都说，前后太守意为前后任太守，多不生出郡城意为大多不居住在郡城之外，表示一种普遍看法。
  • 苟不流死则贬 - 苟不流死则贬意为除非被流放或死亡，否则会被贬职，表示一种后果。
  • 嘉按其图籍 - 嘉是人名，按其图籍意为查阅其记录，表示他查阅了历史记录。
  • 自后周尉迟迥死王事始也 - 自后周尉迟迥死王事始也意为从后周尉迟迥为国事而死开始，表示一个历史参考。
  • 乃为迥立庙，四时享之 - 乃为迥立庙意为为尉迟迥建立庙宇，四时享之意为四季祭祀，表示一种祭祀仪式。
  • 后三年，入拜大金吾 - 后三年意为三年后，入拜大金吾意为被任命为大金吾，表示一种晋升。
  • 到吴竞加以冕服 - 到吴竞是人名，加以冕服意为赐予冕服，表示一种礼仪上的荣誉。
  • 而其后皆荣迁去 - 而其后意为后来，皆荣迁去意为都得到了晋升，表示一系列的晋升。

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 唐初有阚棱者 - Classical introductory phrase meaning 'There was a man named Kan Leng in the early Tang dynasty.'

  • 文帝不忍行法 - Means 'Emperor Wen could not bring himself to enforce the law,' showing the emperor's compassion.

  • 或云 - Means 'some say,' introducing an alternative account.
  • 昭侍饮，酌酒不满，一郎纠之 - Means 'Bo Zhao was attending a drinking party, where he poured wine that was not full, and a junior official criticized him,' providing details of the incident.
  • 帝闻之，怒，故有此 - Means 'the emperor heard about it, became angry, and thus had this [action taken],' explaining the emperor's reaction.
  • 北方有匈奴 - Means 'in the north, there were the Xiongnu,' introducing a historical group.
  • 形质皆人，而足如马蹄 - Means 'their appearance was like humans, but their feet were like horse hooves,' describing the unique physical characteristics of the Xiongnu.
  • 谓之“马蹄突厥” - Means 'they were called 'horse-footed Tujue,' providing the specific name for the group.
  • 窦滔久戍 - Means 'Diu Tao was on long-term military service,' introducing another historical figure.
  • 其妻苏氏能词 - Means 'his wife Su was skilled in poetry,' highlighting her talent.
  • 织锦为回文诗 - Means 'she wove a poem in a circular pattern,' describing the form of her work.
  • 叙离间阻隔之意以寄之 - Means 'to express the feelings of separation and distance,' explaining the theme of her poem.
  • 其理纵横读之皆有旨义 - Means 'when read in any direction, it all makes sense,' emphasizing the cleverness of the poem.
  • 贾达年五岁 - Means 'Jia Da was five years old,' introducing a young historical figure.
  • 姊抱听邻家读书 - Means 'his sister held him while he listened to the neighbors reading,' describing his early education.
  • 及长，俱能通经籍 - Means 'when he grew up, both of them were able to understand the classics,' showing their shared learning.
  • 姊问曰：“吾未尝教汝，何得致然” - Means 'his sister asked, 'I never taught you, how did you learn this?'' introducing a dialogue.
  • 答曰：“姊抱听读书，皆省之。” - Means 'he replied, 'My sister held me while I listened to the neighbors reading, and I understood everything.' explaining his learning process.
  • 及成人，更博群书 - Means 'when he grew up, he read more widely,' showing his continued education.
  • 天下闻名，载粟帛受业，而家大富 - Means 'he became famous throughout the land, people brought grain and silk to study under him, and his family became very wealthy,' describing his success.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 晉荀爽女適陰瑜 - Standard biographical formula meaning 'Jin Xunshuang's daughter married Yin Yu.'
  • 愍其少寡 - Means 'pitied her for being young and widowed.'
  • 欲奪志再嫁郭奕 - Means 'wanted to change her mind and have her remarry Guo Yi.'
  • 遣所亲人问之 - Means 'sent his close relatives to ask her.'
  • 女私挟刃至 - Means 'the woman secretly carried a knife with her.'
  • 爽夺之 - Means 'Xunshuang took it from her.'
  • 广集亲族，设大宴 - Means 'gathered many relatives and set up a grand banquet.'
  • 方合，令奕突出见之 - Means 'just as they were about to start, he ordered Guo Yi to suddenly appear before her.'
  • 女令四角备烛 - Means 'the woman ordered candles to be placed at the four corners.'
  • 与奕相见 - Means 'to meet Guo Yi.'
  • 但危坐 - Means 'he only sat upright.'
  • 即令备浴 - Means 'immediately ordered a bath to be prepared.'
  • 女遣二侍者出家以取他物 - Means 'the woman sent two maidservants out of the house to fetch other things.'
  • 乃刺臂血书扇曰 - Means 'then she pricked her arm and wrote with her blood on a fan, saying...'
  • 以尸还阴氏 - Means 'to return her body to the Yin family.'
  • 自缢而死 - Means 'hanged herself and died.'
  • 昔者愚公居山之阴 - Refers to the ancient fable 'The Foolish Old Man Removes the Mountains,' where the old man lives in the shadow of the mountains.
  • 勖励子孙移之 - Means 'encouraged his descendants to move it.'
  • 山神见曰 - Means 'the mountain god said.'
  • 山极崇高 - Means 'the mountain is extremely high.'
  • 汝何可移 - Means 'how can you move it?'
  • 吾生有子，子复有孙 - Means 'I have children, and they will have grandchildren.'
  • 誓而移之 - Means 'swear to move it.'
  • 何为不可 - Means 'why not?'
  • 于是神命夸娥氏为移之 - Means 'then the god commanded the Qu'e clan to move it.'
  • 秦于骊山之下，坑儒士二百四十人 - Means 'Qin buried 240 scholars alive at Lishan.'
  • 焚诗书，皆用李斯之计 - Means 'burned the *Shi* and *Shu* (Books), all according to Li Si's plan.'
  • 欲愚黔首焉 - Means 'to make the common people ignorant.'
  • 赫连勃勃，本号屈子 - Means 'Helian Bobo, originally known as Qushizi.'
  • 自改其姓云赫连勃勃 - Means 'changed his surname to Helian Bobo, saying 'bright and connected with heaven.'
  • 杀人，积其头为京观 - Means 'killed people, piling their heads into a *jingguan* (trophy heap).'
  • 谓之髑髅台 - Means 'called it the Skull Tower.'
  • 蒸土筑城 - Means 'boiled clay to build a city.'
  • 锥刺入，即杖筑者死 - Means 'if a spearhead penetrated, the builder was beaten to death.'
  • 刺之不入，即锻锥者死 - Means 'if it did not penetrate, the spearhead maker was beaten to death.'
  • 其造器，射入甲者，杀铠匠 - Means 'in making weapons, if an arrow penetrated armor, the armor maker was killed.'
  • 不入者，杀弓匠 - Means 'if it did not penetrate, the bow maker was killed.'
  • 莫知所措 - Means 'no one knew what to do.'

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 聪明多才思 - Intelligent and full of talent and thought.
  • 武帝出千言，无章句，令嗣次之，因成《千字文》 - Emperor Wu produced a thousand words without chapters or sentences, ordered Zhou Xingsi to arrange them, and thus created the Thousand Character Classic.
  • 归而两目俱丧 - Upon returning, both his eyes were lost.
  • 及死，开视之，心如掬燥泥 - Upon death, upon opening it, the heart was like scooping dry mud.
  • 刘幽求自朝邑尉为中书舍人，三日内拜相 - Liu Youqiu, from the position of magistrate of Chaoyi, became a Secretary of the Central Secretariat and was appointed chancellor within three days.
  • 太宗谓虞世南一人有五绝 - Emperor Taizong said that Yu Shiniu has five excellences.
  • 一曰博闻，二曰德行，三曰书翰，四曰词藻，五曰忠直 - The first is broad knowledge, the second is virtue and conduct, the third is calligraphy and writing, the fourth is rhetoric and style, the fifth is loyalty and integrity.
  • 图形凌烟，寿年八十一终 - Had his image painted in the Hall of Bright Clouds and lived to the age of eighty-one and died.
  • 公孙瓒既杀刘虞，则见有胜己者皆杀之 - After killing Liu Yu, then saw those who were superior to him and killed them all.
  • 男子年七岁者，不许入城 - Men aged seven were not allowed to enter the city.
  • 传达皆用妇人，令语音雄者宣扬其命 - To convey, all used women, ordered those with strong voices to proclaim his commands.
  • 袁绍逼急，乃先缢其妻儿姊妹，然后自赴火而死 - In dire straits, then first hanged his wife, children, and sisters, then threw himself into the fire and died.
  • 魏武残人性命，重伎艺 - Cao Cao was cruel to human life but valued skills.
  • 有一歌儿性甚慧，而声响入云 - There was a singing boy who was very intelligent and whose voice could reach the clouds.
  • 操爱其声未忍杀，乃于群妾中求得二人，声如歌者，密令教授，数月乃成 - Cao Cao loved his voice and did not dare to kill him, then sought out two women among his concubines whose voices were like his, secretly ordered them to teach him, and after several months they succeeded.
  • 听之，立杀其前者 - After hearing it, immediately killed the previous ones.
  • 范粲，字承明 - Fan Can, style name Chengming.
  • 时齐王芳被废，承明哭甚恸，因不仕 - When Prince Fang of Qi was deposed, Chengming wept bitterly and therefore did not take office.
  • 景王辅政，召之不赴，称疾，佯狂不言，足不履地，人不闻音 - When Sima Shi assisted in governance, when summoned did not respond, claimed illness, pretended madness and did not speak, feet did not touch the ground, people did not hear his voice.
  • 家有婚姻，咨访之，合意者，即色不变；否者，即卧寝不安 - When there were marriages in the family, consulted him, if they agreed, his expression did not change; if they disagreed, he would not sleep peacefully.
  • 家人以此候其旨于所寝之车 - Family members gauged his wishes from the carriage where he slept.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 除去淫祠一千二百所 - Removed 1,200 superfluous shrines; 淫祠 refers to unauthorized or excessive shrines, often built for local deities or spirits.
  • 每战，喊一声，即雁数行飞逆 - Every time he shouted, geese flew in rows against him; metaphor for the psychological impact of Zhang Xun's leadership, where his cries were so powerful that they could even affect birds.
  • 籍哭，即呕血，毁瘠骨立 - Jue cried, and vomited blood, becoming emaciated and standing like a skeleton; vividly describes the intense grief and physical toll of mourning.
  • 归而刻木为人，书曰：“酒徒蒋济”，以弓矢射之 - Returning home, he carved a figure out of wood, wrote 'drunkard Jiang Ji,' and shot it with an arrow; symbolic act of revenge and a critique of Jiang Ji's behavior.
  • 牧长闻之，不能制 - The local officials heard about it and could not control him; indicates the power and influence of the individuals involved.
  • 家无衣帛之妾，枥无食粟之马 - He had no concubines dressed in silk, and no horses fed on grain; emphasizes Duke Ji's frugality and moral integrity.
  • 其先有妇人名沙壹，居哀牢山 - Her ancestors were a woman named Sha Yi, who lived in Ailao Mountain; introduces the mythological origin of the Ailao tribe.
  • 今西南夷，其裔也 - The present-day southwestern barbarians are her descendants; connects the myth to the current population.
  • 获封邑不因夫子者，葬用卤簿，自此始 - The first case of a woman receiving a fiefdom without being a wife of a noble, and being buried with a full ceremonial escort; highlights a significant change in social norms.
  • 贤可恃乎！ - Can one rely on virtue?; rhetorical question challenging the assumption that virtue alone is sufficient for good governance.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 常有疾 - Chang Bo is ill, where '常' is a proper name, and '有疾' means 'is ill.'
  • 老子曰 - Laozi said, where '老子' refers to Laozi, the founder of Daoism.
  • 能无教语弟子乎 - Can you not teach your disciples? This is a rhetorical question implying that Chang Bo should impart wisdom before his death.
  • 大其口示老子 - He opened his mouth wide for Laozi to see. This gesture is symbolic and can be interpreted as a metaphor for revealing something important.
  • 岂非柔耶 - Is it not soft? This rhetorical question implies that the tongue's softness is its strength.
  • 齿亡乎 - Are your teeth gone? This rhetorical question implies that the teeth's hardness is their weakness.
  • 天下事尽矣 - All things in the world are complete. This statement suggests that the lesson of flexibility and adaptability is universal.
  • 子产闻妇人哭 - Zichan heard a woman crying. '子产' refers to Zichan, a statesman of the State of Zheng.
  • 使人执而拘之 - He had someone arrest her. This indicates a formal action taken by Zichan.
  • 果手刃其夫者 - It turned out she was the one who killed her husband. This reveals the outcome of the investigation.
  • 御者问曰 - The driver asked, where '御者' refers to the charioteer or driver.
  • 何以知之 - How did you know? This question seeks the reasoning behind Zichan's conclusion.
  • 夫人所亲也 - She is the one who is close to him. This refers to the woman's relationship with her husband.
  • 有病则忧 - When he is sick, she worries. This describes the typical reaction of a loving wife.
  • 临死则哀 - When he dies, she mourns. This describes the natural response to a loved one's death.
  • 不哀而惧 - She does not mourn but fears. This indicates a deviation from the expected behavior.
  • 是以知有奸也 - Therefore, I knew there was something wrong. This explains Zichan's reasoning.
  • 隋朝徐德言妻陈氏 - In the Sui Dynasty, Xu Deyan's wife Chen Shi. This introduces the historical context and characters.
  • 叔宝妹 - Sister of Shubao, where '叔宝' refers to Emperor Wen of Sui, whose sister was Chen Shi.
  • 惧乱不能相保 - Afraid of chaos and unable to protect each other. This describes the fear of separation during turbulent times.
  • 破一镜分之 - He broke a mirror and divided it. This act symbolizes a promise to reunite in the future.
  • 以为他年不知存亡 - To ensure they would know each other's fate in the future. This explains the purpose of breaking the mirror.
  • 端午日各持其半镜于市内卖之 - On the fifth day of the fifth month, they each held half of the mirror and sold it in the market. This sets up the reunion plan.
  • 以图相合 - To try to reunite. This describes the goal of the plan.
  • 至期适市 - On the appointed day, he went to the market. This describes Xu Deyan's action.
  • 果有一破镜 - He found a broken mirror. This describes the unexpected discovery.
  • 德言乃题其背曰 - Xu Deyan then wrote on the back of it. This describes his action.
  • 镜与人俱去 - The mirror and the person went together. This line reflects the separation.
  • 镜归人不归 - The mirror returns, but the person does not. This line reflects the current situation.
  • 无复嫦娥影 - There is no longer the shadow of Chang'e. This metaphor suggests the loss of the beloved.
  • 空余半月辉 - Only the half-moon's light remains. This metaphor suggests the remaining hope of reunion.
  • 时陈氏为杨素所爱 - At that time, Chen Shi was loved by Yang Su. This provides the background of Chen Shi's situation.
  • 见之，乃命德言对饮 - When she saw it, she ordered Xu Deyan to drink with her. This describes the meeting.
  • 三人环坐 - They sat in a circle. This describes the seating arrangement.
  • 令陈氏赋诗一章 - She ordered Chen Shi to compose a poem. This describes the request.
  • 即还之 - And then returned it to him. This describes the return of the mirror.
  • 陈氏诗曰 - Chen Shi's poem said, where '陈氏' refers to Chen Shi.
  • 今日何迁次 - Today, what has changed? This line reflects the unexpected turn of events.
  • 新官对旧官 - A new official faces an old official. This metaphor suggests the change in status.
  • 笑啼俱不敢 - Neither smiling nor crying, she dared not. This describes her emotional state.
  • 方验作人难 - Only now does she realize how difficult it is to be a person. This line reflects her realization of the complexities of human life.
  • 素感之，乃还德言 - Yang Su was moved by this and returned the mirror to Xu Deyan. This describes the outcome of the encounter.

  • 四十六日不出 - Literally 'did not emerge for forty-six days,' referring to the duration of the star's absence.
  • 天帝怒，命五岳搜捕焉 - Means 'The Heavenly Emperor was angered and ordered the Five Sacred Mountains to search for them,' a mythological event.

  • 辄回驭 - Means 'would always turn back,' describing the rain god's behavior.
  • 故此地常少雨焉 - Means 'thus this place often lacks rain,' summarizing the consequence of the rain god's actions.
  • 淮南有居客卢婴者 - Means 'In Huainan, there was a guest named Lu Ying,' introducing a new character.
  • 气质文学，俱为郡中绝 - Means 'His character and literary talent were both outstanding in the prefecture,' praising his qualities.
  • 甚奇蹇 - Means 'very strange and unlucky,' describing his peculiar fate.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sequence '隋文帝未贵时，常舟行江中。夜泊中，梦无左手。及觉，甚恶之。' must be read as a series of events: the emperor's habitual boat travel, his night stop, his dream, and his reaction.
  • The sequence '登岸，诣一草庵。中有一老僧，道极高。具以梦告之。僧起贺曰：“无左手者，独拳也。当为天子。”' must be read as a series of actions: landing, visiting a hut, meeting a monk, telling the dream, and receiving a prediction.
  • The sequence '兴建此庵为吉祥寺。居武昌下三十里。' must be read as a transformation and a location description.
  • The sequence '贞元中，吴郡进士李赤者，与赵敏之相同游闽。行及衢之信安，去县三十里，宿于馆厅。' must be read as a series of events: the time period, the scholars' journey, their stop, and their lodging.
  • The sequence '宵分，忽有一妇人入庭中。赤于睡中蹶起下阶，与之揖让。良久即上厅，开箧取纸笔，作一书与其亲，词旨重叠，讫，乃封于箧中。复下庭，妇人抽其巾缢之。敏之走出大叫，妇人乃收巾而走。及视其书，如赤梦中所为。' must be read as a series of events: the unexpected visit, the scholar's actions, the writing of the letter, the violent act, and the discovery.
  • The sequence '明日，又偕行。南次建中驿，白昼又失赤。敏之即遽往厕，见赤坐于床，大怒敏之曰：“方当礼谢，为尔所惊。”浃日至闽，属寮有与赤游旧者，设宴饮次，又失赤。敏之疾索于厕，见赤僵仆于地，气已绝矣。' must be read as a series of events: the continuation of the journey, another disappearance, the search, and the fatal outcome.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 附录 - Refers to an appendix or supplementary material.
  • 尝造飞楼七十间 - Means 'once built seventy flying pavilions,' where '飞楼' refers to elevated or multi-tiered structures.
  • 令妓女走马于其上 - Describes a scene where courtesans were made to gallop on top of these structures for entertainment.
  • 以为戏乐 - Means 'for entertainment.'
  • 怆然有思 - Means 'felt sorrowful and reflective.'
  • 乃觉其妻在帐外 - Means 'he then felt his wife was outside the tent.'
  • 愍君涉海 - Means 'I pity you for crossing the sea.'
  • 志愿奔而随之 - Means 'and I wish to follow you.'
  • 人无知者 - Means 'no one knows about it.'
  • 欲纳一妓 - Means 'he wants to take in a courtesan.'
  • 将侍枕席 - Means 'to serve him in bed.'
  • 无怪者 - Means 'no one found it strange.'
  • 隐乃启舅姑 - Means 'Wei Yin then informed his in-laws.'
  • 首其罪 - Means 'confessing his sin.'
  • 而室中宛存焉 - Means 'but the room remained as it was.'
  • 及相近 - Means 'when they came close.'
  • 翕然合体 - Means 'they merged into one body.'
  • 其从隐者 - Means 'the one who followed him.'
  • 乃魂也 - Means 'was a spirit.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 唐 - Refers to the Tang Dynasty (618–907 CE), a period of great cultural and political achievement in Chinese history.
  • 许敬宗 - A prominent official during the Tang Dynasty known for his political influence and luxurious lifestyle.
  • 大历 - A reign title of Emperor Dezong of Tang, covering the years 766–779 CE.
  • 新罗 - An ancient Korean kingdom that was a significant cultural and political entity in East Asia during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 尚衣奉御 - A high-ranking official responsible for the emperor's clothing and attire.
  • 魂 - Refers to a spirit or ghost, a concept deeply rooted in Chinese beliefs about the afterlife and spiritual existence.

Proper Nouns

  • 唐许敬宗 - Xu Jingzong, a high-ranking official during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 大历 - The reign title of Emperor Dezong of Tang.
  • 韩晋卿 - Han Jinqing, a craftsman during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 韦隐 - Wei Yin, a court official responsible for the emperor's clothing.
  • 新罗 - Silla, an ancient Korean kingdom.
  • 魂 - Spirit or ghost.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 乳母抱行 - Literal phrase meaning 'the wet nurse carried him,' implying the child was very young.
  • 东邻树孔中探得金环 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'found a golden ring in a hole in the neighbor's tree,' symbolizing an unusual and significant event.
  • 吾儿七岁堕井死 - Literal phrase meaning 'my son fell into a well and died at seven years old,' indicating a tragic event.
  • 曾弄金环，失其处所 - Literal phrase meaning 'he once played with a golden ring and lost its location,' suggesting the ring was significant to the child.
  • 验祜前身 - Phrase meaning 'verify his previous life,' indicating a belief in reincarnation.
  • 东邻子也 - Phrase meaning 'he was the son of the neighbor,' confirming the identity of the child in his previous life.
  • 泛洞庭 - Literal phrase meaning 'sail across Dongting Lake,' indicating a journey.
  • 鼻衄，血沙上 - Literal phrase meaning 'nosebleed, blood on the sand,' describing a physical symptom.
  • 为江鼍所舐 - Phrase meaning 'was licked by a river crocodile,' indicating a supernatural encounter.
  • 俄然复生一鹬 - Phrase meaning 'suddenly a new Ruyu was born,' describing a miraculous event.
  • 其形体衣服言语，与其身无异 - Phrase meaning 'its body, clothing, and speech were no different from his,' indicating a complete transformation.
  • 为鼍法所制，絷于水中 - Phrase meaning 'was bound by the crocodile's magic and tied in the water,' indicating a state of captivity.
  • 妻子家人迎奉鼍妖就任 - Phrase meaning 'his wife, children, and family welcomed the crocodile demon to take office,' indicating a state of acceptance.
  • 州人亦不能觉悟 - Phrase meaning 'the people of the state could not realize,' indicating a lack of awareness.
  • 天下大旱，西江可涉 - Phrase meaning 'a great drought, the West River could be crossed,' indicating a significant natural event.
  • 道士叶静能自罗浮山赴玄宗急诏 - Phrase meaning 'the Taoist Ye Jingneng from Luofu Mountain came to answer the urgent edict of Emperor Xuanzong,' indicating a religious figure responding to a royal command.
  • 忽沙中见一人面缚 - Phrase meaning 'suddenly saw a man bound face down in the sand,' indicating a supernatural encounter.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 晋羊祜 - Refers to Yang Hu, a prominent official during the Jin Dynasty.
  • 乳母 - Refers to a wet nurse, a common practice in ancient China.
  • 东邻 - Refers to a neighbor, indicating a close community relationship.
  • 金环 - A golden ring, often symbolizing wealth or status.
  • 鼍 - A river crocodile, often associated with water spirits or demons in Chinese folklore.
  • 李鹬 - Refers to Li Ruyu, a historical figure during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 开元 - Refers to the Kaiyuan era, a period of prosperity during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 邵州 - Refers to a region in Hunan Province.
  • 洞庭 - Refers to Dongting Lake, one of the largest freshwater lakes in China.
  • 玄宗 - Refers to Emperor Xuanzong of Tang, a famous emperor known for his cultural achievements.
  • 罗浮山 - Refers to Luofu Mountain, a sacred mountain in Guangdong Province.
  • 道士 - Refers to a Taoist priest, indicating a religious figure.
  • 叶静能 - Refers to Ye Jingneng, a famous Taoist priest during the Tang Dynasty.
